---
title: Demo
format: ipynb
filters: 
    - path: div-reuse
      at: post-quarto
---

I expect this .qmd when rendered to ipynb to contain two Markdown and two code cells.

::: {#fig-test}

```{python}
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

x = np.linspace(0, 10, 100)
y = np.sin(x)

plt.plot(x, y)
plt.title("Sine Wave")
```

Some caption!

:::

## Bird stuff

```{python}
import librosa
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

call, sr = librosa.load("../data/birdcall/downy-woodpecker/Downy Woodpecker (4).mp3")

plt.plot(call)

```

```{python}
melspectrogram = librosa.feature.melspectrogram(y=call, sr=sr, n_mels=128, fmax=8000)
plt.imshow(melspectrogram, aspect="auto", origin="lower", zorder = 10, cmap = "inferno", vmin = 0, vmax = 50)
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import os 

from osxmetadata import OSXMetaData
from osxmetadata import kMDItemAuthors


base = "../data/birdcall/downy-woodpecker/"
fnames = os.listdir(base)

for fname in fnames:
  # get the author metadata of the file

  path = os.path.join(base, fname)
  meta = OSXMetaData(path)
  # print(meta.asdict())
  try: 
    print(meta.authors)
  except: 
    pass
    
```

## back to Xenocanto

```{python}
import requests

def query(species, max_length,key):
    query_url = f'https://xeno-canto.org/api/3/recordings?query=sp:"{species}"+len:"<{max_length}"&key={key}'
    response = requests.get(query_url)
    data = response.json()
    return data

species_list = ["dryobates pubescens", "melanerpes carolinus"]

for species in species_list:
    os.mkdir(f"../data/birdcall/{species}")
    q = query(species, 10, key)
    for recording in q["recordings"]:
        print(recording["file"])
        # download the file if the filename includes mp3
        if recording["file-name"].endswith(".mp3"):
            response = requests.get(recording["file"])
            with open(f"../data/birdcall/{species}/{recording['id']}.mp3", "wb") as file:
                file.write(response.content)
```

```{python}
os.mkdir("downy-woodpecker")
for recording in downy_query["recordings"]:
    print(recording["file"])
    # download the file if the filename includes mp3
    if recording["file-name"].endswith(".mp3"):
        response = requests.get(recording["file"])
        with open(f"downy-woodpecker/{recording['id']}.mp3", "wb") as file:
            file.write(response.content)
```


```{python}
# # query for downy woodpecker calls shorter than 10 seconds
downy_query = 'https://xeno-canto.org/api/3/recordings?query=sp:"dryobates pubescens"+len:"<10"&key=98a0381641845bbb25fecfd70adf7dff582e40e5'

response = requests.get(downy_query)
data = response.json()
print(data)

# # query for Melanerpes carolinus (red-bellied woodpecker) calls shorter than 10 seconds

# red_bellied_query = 'https://xeno-canto.org/api/3/recordings?query=sp:"melanerpes carolinus"+len:"<10"&key=98a0381641845bbb25fecfd70adf7dff582e40e5'

# response = requests.get(red_bellied_query)
# data = response.json()
# print(data)





```
